entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The host crop being bred for resistance to Striga hermonthica

entities.name: STRIGA HERMONTHICA
entities.description: A parasitic weed that attacks sorghum

entities.name: STRIGA RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum to resist infection and damage by Striga hermonthica

entities.name: INDIRECT AND DIRECT SELECTION TRAITS
entities.description: Various measures used as markers to select for Striga resistance

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED POPULATIONS
entities.description: Populations developed from specific crosses to study genetic resistance

entities.name: IS 9830
entities.description: A Striga-resistant sorghum line characterized by low stimulation of seed germination

entities.name: E 36-1
entities.description: A Striga-susceptible sorghum line that produces germination stimulants in abundance

entities.name: N 13
entities.description: A sorghum line possessing mechanical resistance and likely an antibiosis mechanism

entities.name: LOW STIMULATION OF STRIGA SEED GERMINATION
entities.description: A resistance characteristic where the host does not trigger Striga seed germination

entities.name: GERMINATION STIMULANTS
entities.description: Chemicals produced by the host plant that trigger the germination of Striga seeds

entities.name: MECHANICAL RESISTANCE
entities.description: A physical barrier or mechanism that prevents Striga parasitism

entities.name: ANTIBIOSIS MECHANISM
entities.description: A chemical mechanism that inhibits the growth or survival of the parasite

entities.name: AGAR-GEL ASSAY
entities.description: An in vitro screening method used to identify sorghum entries with low-stimulant characters

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: A country where pot trials and field experiments were conducted

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: A country where pot trials and field experiments were conducted

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: A country where pot trials were conducted

entities.name: POT TRIALS
entities.description: A screening method used to evaluate Striga resistance in a controlled environment

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The evaluation of Striga resistance under actual field conditions

entities.name: LOW-STIMULANT CHARACTER
entities.description: The trait of producing few or no germination stimulants

entities.name: CORRELATION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to determine the relationship between indirect traits and field resistance

entities.name: HERITABILITY ESTIMATES
entities.description: A measure of the proportion of phenotypic variation due to genetic factors

entities.name: EMERGED STRIGA COUNTS
entities.description: A direct measure of Striga resistance based on the number of emerged weeds

entities.name: STRIGA SEVERITY INDEX
entities.description: A direct measure of the impact of Striga on the host plant

entities.name: AREA UNDER THE STRIGA NUMBER OR SEVERITY PROGRESS CURVES
entities.description: A direct measure of Striga resistance integrating the progress of infection over time

entities.name: TWO-ROW PLOT FIELD LAYOUT
entities.description: A specific field arrangement used to improve screening precision

entities.name: ARTIFICIAL INFESTATION
entities.description: The process of intentionally introducing Striga seeds into test rows

entities.name: LATTICE DESIGN
entities.description: An experimental design used in field trials to reduce environmental variance

entities.name: GENOTYPE X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTIONS
entities.description: The interaction between the genetic makeup of the plant and the environment affecting resistance

entities.name: MULTI-LOCATIONAL TRIALS
entities.description: Trials conducted across different locations to ensure stable resistance
